# Banana Clean — Gemini Watermark Remover

> Banana Clean is a free Chrome extension that removes the Gemini watermark (Nano Banana / Nano Banana Pro logo) from AI-generated images automatically when you click Download. Lossless local processing in under 100ms. No uploads, no sign-ups.

---

## What Banana Clean Does

Banana Clean is a browser extension that intercepts image downloads from gemini.google.com and aistudio.google.com. When you click the Download button on any AI-generated image, the extension automatically removes the visible Nano Banana watermark and saves a clean file to your downloads folder.

The extension works in Chrome, Edge, Opera, and Brave — any Chromium-based browser that supports extensions from the Chrome Web Store.

## How the Watermark Removal Algorithm Works

Banana Clean uses reverse alpha blending to restore the original pixel values underneath the watermark. The formula:

```
original = (watermarked - alpha * logo) / (1 - alpha)
```

The extension ships with pre-computed masks of the Nano Banana logo at two sizes: 48x48 pixels (standard resolution images) and 96x96 pixels (high resolution images). When you download an image, the extension reads the watermark region, applies the inverse formula pixel by pixel using the Canvas API, and restores the original colors.

This is pure math — no AI inpainting, no machine learning, no server processing. The result is lossless: the cleaned image is pixel-identical to what the image would look like without the watermark.

Processing time: under 100ms per image.

Manual Gemini watermark remover: https://banana-clean.app/manual-cleanup

The manual cleanup workflow is for images where a small Gemini or Nano Banana watermark trace remains after automatic cleanup. The user loads an image, paints the affected area with a brush, runs local cleanup in the browser, reviews the original and cleaned files in the result card, and can explicitly choose whether to send the original image and mask to Banana Clean for sample analysis.

Dedicated image pages:

- Gemini Watermark Remover Online: https://banana-clean.app/gemini-watermark-remover
- Nano Banana Watermark Remover: https://banana-clean.app/nano-banana-watermark-remover

These pages connect the automatic extension, the manual browser cleaner, and image-specific search intent. They explain when to use automatic Gemini download cleanup, when to open Manual Cleanup for a saved file, and when OMNI Video is the right path for supported MP4 downloads.

OMNI Video cleanup: https://banana-clean.app/blog/gemini-flow-video-watermark-cleanup

Banana Clean has a separate OMNI Video cleanup workflow for supported Gemini and Google Flow MP4 downloads. It targets visible video marks in short AI clips. Image cleanup and video cleanup are separate plans because video has many frames and takes longer to process. Banana Clean removes visible marks only and does not remove SynthID or invisible provenance systems.

## SynthID vs Visible Watermark

Google embeds two types of watermarks into Gemini-generated images:

1. **Visible watermark (Nano Banana logo)**: A small logo in the bottom-right corner. This is what Banana Clean removes.
2. **SynthID (invisible)**: An imperceptible watermark embedded into the pixel data by Google DeepMind. SynthID survives cropping, resizing, and most image editing.

Banana Clean removes only the visible watermark. SynthID cannot be removed by any tool without destroying image quality. Banana Clean does not attempt to remove SynthID.

## Supported Websites

- **gemini.google.com** — Google Gemini chat with image generation (Nano Banana and Nano Banana Pro models)
- **aistudio.google.com** — Google AI Studio with the same image generation models

## How to Use

1. Install Banana Clean from the Chrome Web Store
2. Open gemini.google.com or aistudio.google.com
3. Generate an image as usual
4. Click the Download button — the extension intercepts the download automatically
5. The clean image (without the Nano Banana watermark) is saved to your downloads folder

## Pricing

- **Free tier**: 15 watermark removals per install, no sign-up or credit card required
- **PRO monthly**: Unlimited image watermark removals, export tools, and crop presets from $2.99/month.
- **PRO yearly**: Same image access for $24.99/year.
- **OMNI Video**: Separate 30-day plan for supported Gemini MP4 files, $9.99 or 500 Telegram Stars.
- Payment via Telegram Stars or Plisio crypto checkout while card checkout is unavailable.

## Who Uses Banana Clean

- **Content creators**: Publish AI-generated visuals to social media, blogs, and newsletters without the Gemini logo
- **Designers**: Use Gemini images in mockups, presentations, and client work — watermark-free
- **Marketers**: Create ad creatives, landing page visuals, and product shots from AI Studio without visible branding
- **Developers**: Generate placeholder images, app assets, and documentation graphics clean and ready to ship

## Banana Clean vs Online Watermark Removers

Online watermark removal tools (like media.io, vmake.ai, visualgpt.io) require you to upload your image to a third-party server. They use AI inpainting which can introduce artifacts and takes several seconds per image.

Banana Clean is different:
- Runs as a browser extension — no manual uploading
- Processes images locally in your browser — no privacy risk
- Uses exact reverse math (not AI guessing) — lossless result
- Takes under 100ms — vs several seconds for online tools
- Works automatically — intercepts the download button, no extra steps

## Frequently Asked Questions

### What is the Gemini watermark (Nano Banana)?
Nano Banana is Google's image generation model built into Gemini and AI Studio. It adds a small banana logo to the bottom-right corner of every generated image — 48x48 or 96x96 pixels depending on resolution.

### How do I remove the Gemini watermark?
Install Banana Clean from the Chrome Web Store. The extension intercepts downloads from Gemini and AI Studio and removes the watermark automatically. Generate an image, click Download, get a clean file.

### Is Banana Clean free?
You get 15 free cleanings — no card, no sign-up. After that, PRO unlocks unlimited image cleanup and export tools from $2.99/month or $24.99/year. OMNI Video is a separate 30-day plan for supported Gemini MP4 files.

### Which browsers are supported?
Chrome, Edge, Opera, and Brave — any Chromium-based browser that supports extensions from the Chrome Web Store.

### Does it upload my images to a server?
No. Watermark removal runs entirely in your browser using the Canvas API. Your images never leave your machine.

### Does Banana Clean remove SynthID?
No. SynthID is an invisible watermark embedded in pixel data by Google DeepMind. It cannot be removed without destroying image quality. Banana Clean only removes the visible Nano Banana logo.

### Does it work with Nano Banana Pro images?
Yes. Nano Banana Pro uses the same visible watermark. Banana Clean removes it from both standard and Pro variants.

### Can I remove the watermark from Google AI Studio images?
Yes. Banana Clean works on both gemini.google.com and aistudio.google.com. AI Studio uses the same Nano Banana watermark.

### How does Banana Clean remove the watermark technically?
Reverse alpha blending: original = (watermarked - α × logo) / (1 - α). Pre-computed masks at 48x48 and 96x96 pixels. Pure math via Canvas API — no AI, no inpainting.

### Does it work with "Save Image As" or right-click save?
No. Banana Clean intercepts the official Download button in Gemini and AI Studio. Right-click "Save Image As" saves the in-chat preview which may be lower resolution.

### What if Google changes the watermark?
Watermark masks are bundled with the extension and updated through the Chrome Web Store. New pattern — new update.

## Privacy

- Images are processed locally in the browser and never sent to any server
- The extension collects only anonymous usage events for product analytics (install, active day, clean count)
- No logins, passwords, chat content, or personal data from Google accounts
- No data is sold to third parties
- Telegram Stars payments are handled inside Telegram. Plisio crypto payments are confirmed through hosted invoice webhooks.

## Blog

### Gemini and Google Flow Video Watermark Cleanup

URL: https://banana-clean.app/blog/gemini-flow-video-watermark-cleanup

Banana Clean supports OMNI Video cleanup for supported Gemini and Google Flow MP4 downloads. The extension catches supported video downloads, cleans the visible mark across frames, and saves the cleaned file. It is meant for Gemini app videos, Google Flow 720p exports, and short concept clips for social posts, ad drafts, presentations and client previews.

Video cleanup takes longer than image cleanup because a clip has many frames. Short 720p clips can take about a minute on a normal desktop browser. 1080p can work, but it needs more local processing time. Banana Clean does not crop or zoom the video corner as a shortcut. It keeps the frame and removes the visible mark when it can detect it.

OMNI Video does not remove SynthID or invisible AI provenance. It targets visible marks in supported Gemini and Flow MP4 downloads.

---

### Manual AI Watermark Cleanup for Images

URL: https://banana-clean.app/blog/manual-ai-watermark-cleanup

Manual Cleanup is the browser-based AI cleanup flow for saved images, shifted Gemini marks, faint Nano Banana leftovers, small logos, captions, date stamps and small unwanted objects. The user loads an image, brushes over the area to fix, runs cleanup locally in the browser, compares the result and downloads the cleaned PNG.

Automatic cleanup is the best path for fresh Gemini and AI Studio downloads. Manual Cleanup is better when the image is already saved, the visible mark moved, or a faint trace remains on hard backgrounds such as marble, leather, fabric, sky, water or dark product shots.

The cleanup model runs locally. Sample sharing is optional and requires explicit consent before sending the original image and mask to Banana Clean for product improvement.

---

### How to Remove the Gemini Watermark (Nano Banana) from Images

URL: https://banana-clean.app/blog/how-to-remove-gemini-watermark

Every image generated by Google Gemini is stamped with a visible Nano Banana watermark — a semi-transparent banana logo in the bottom-right corner, 48x48 or 96x96 pixels depending on resolution. This is separate from SynthID, Google's invisible watermark.

Three methods to remove it:

1. Manual removal in Photoshop/GIMP: Use Clone Stamp or Content-Aware Fill. Works but slow (2-5 min per image) and the result is an approximation — you are guessing what the original pixels looked like.

2. Online watermark removers (watermarkremover.io, cleanup.pictures, etc.): Upload your image, AI inpaints the marked region. Problems: privacy risk (image uploaded to third-party server), inconsistent quality on detailed backgrounds, some add their own watermarks.

3. Reverse alpha blending (Banana Clean): The Nano Banana watermark uses standard alpha compositing with a known, static mask. The formula to reverse it: original = (watermarked - alpha * 255) / (1 - alpha). This is mathematically exact — no guessing, no inpainting. Maximum error: 1/255 per channel per pixel (invisible). Banana Clean applies this formula automatically when you click Download in Gemini or AI Studio. Processing: under 100ms, fully local in the browser.

Comparison: Photoshop (2-5 min, approximation, local, free/$$23/mo) vs Online tools (30-60 sec, inpainting, upload required, free/$10+/mo) vs Banana Clean (<100ms automatic, lossless, local, 15 free then $4.99 once).

---

### What Is SynthID? How Google Watermarks Gemini Images

URL: https://banana-clean.app/blog/what-is-synthid-watermark

Every Gemini image carries two watermarks: the visible Nano Banana logo (removable) and SynthID (invisible, permanent).

SynthID was developed by Google DeepMind (announced August 2023). It modifies the image during the diffusion generation process — not applied as an overlay afterward. The modifications are sub-pixel-level (less than 1/255 per channel), invisible to humans. The signal is spread across the entire image in the spatial frequency domain, so it survives cropping (even 50%), resizing, JPEG compression at quality 70, and screenshot-reupload cycles. Detection accuracy stays above 95% under normal transformations.

SynthID detection is probabilistic: "detected," "not detected," or "inconclusive." The exact encoding algorithm is proprietary.

Why SynthID cannot be removed: unlike the visible watermark (fixed position, known mask, standard alpha blending), SynthID touches every pixel, adapts to each image's content, and the parameters are secret. Adding enough noise to break the signal would visibly destroy the image.

SynthID also applies to text (biasing token selection), audio, and video from Google models.

Comparison with other platforms: Google uses both visible + invisible watermarks. OpenAI DALL-E uses C2PA metadata only (removed visible watermark in 2024). Midjourney uses undisclosed steganography. Adobe Firefly uses Content Credentials (C2PA). Stability AI varies by deployment.

Banana Clean removes only the visible Nano Banana logo. SynthID remains intact after cleaning.

---

### Can You Use Gemini Images Commercially? Copyright and Watermark Rules

URL: https://banana-clean.app/blog/gemini-images-copyright-commercial-use

Google's Generative AI Additional Terms of Service allow commercial use of Gemini-generated images, subject to content policies (no violence, deception, illegal content). Google does not claim ownership of AI-generated output. No clause limits use to personal or non-commercial.

Copyright gray area: U.S. Copyright Office has repeatedly stated AI-generated images without meaningful human authorship cannot be copyrighted (Zarya of the Dawn case, 2023; additional guidance 2024-2025). You can use Gemini images commercially, but you cannot claim exclusive ownership or stop others from using similar images.

The visible Nano Banana watermark is branding, not a legal mechanism. Google's ToS do not explicitly prohibit removing it. The actual provenance tracking relies on SynthID (invisible, persistent). Removing the visible logo does not violate ToS.

Generally safe uses: blog illustrations, social media, presentations, mockups, thumbnails, newsletters. More caution needed: selling prints/merchandise as primary product, using as brand identity, stock photo contributions. Avoid: claiming AI images are human-made, mimicking specific artists' styles for sale.

Disclosure requirements are expanding: EU AI Act requires AI content labeling. Meta, TikTok, YouTube have AI content disclosure policies. SynthID enables automated enforcement even after visible watermark removal.

Free and paid Gemini tiers have the same commercial usage rights. This differs from some competitors (Midjourney restricts free-tier commercial use).

Not legal advice — consult an attorney for high-stakes decisions.

---

### Google AI Studio Watermark: How to Remove It from Generated Images

URL: https://banana-clean.app/blog/google-ai-studio-watermark

Google AI Studio (aistudio.google.com) uses the same Nano Banana watermark as Gemini. Both run the same underlying model and post-processing pipeline.

Key differences from Gemini for watermark removal: AI Studio outputs are often higher resolution (up to 2048x2048), so the 96x96 mask is used more often. AI Studio also offers API access for programmatic image generation.

For the web UI: Banana Clean works automatically on aistudio.google.com the same way it works on gemini.google.com. Install the extension, generate an image, click Download — the file is already clean.

For API-generated images: Banana Clean cannot help (the extension intercepts browser downloads, not API calls). Options: (1) Apply reverse alpha blending yourself using Pillow (Python) or Sharp (Node.js) with the known mask files. (2) Use a headless browser with the extension loaded. (3) Use the web UI for clean outputs, API for cases where watermark does not matter.

Python implementation for programmatic removal:
```
from PIL import Image
import numpy as np
# Load image and mask, compute alpha = max(R,G,B)/255
# Apply: original = (watermarked - alpha*255) / (1 - alpha) to bottom-right corner
```

Removing the visible watermark does not affect SynthID. Both JPEG and PNG outputs carry the watermark. The watermark is applied server-side before delivery.

---

### Nano Banana Pro vs Nano Banana 2: Watermarks and How to Remove Them

URL: https://banana-clean.app/blog/nano-banana-pro-watermark

Google has released four versions of the Nano Banana image generation model:

- Nano Banana (early 2025): First version. 1024x1024 max. Basic quality. Gemini Free + AI Studio.
- Nano Banana Pro (November 2025): Better detail, up to 1536px. Gemini Advanced + AI Studio.
- Nano Banana 2 (January 2026): Replaced original on free tier. Better text rendering, fewer artifacts. 1024px max.
- Nano Banana 2 Pro (February 2026): Current flagship. Up to 2048x2048. Photo-realistic mode. Gemini Advanced + AI Studio.

The visible watermark is identical across all versions: same banana-shaped logo, same bottom-right position, same alpha blending formula, same opacity (~15-20%). The only variable is size: images up to ~1024px get 48x48 mask, larger images get 96x96 mask. Since newer models output at higher resolutions, you see the 96px version more often.

Free tier vs Gemini Advanced: no watermark difference. Google applies the same visible watermark to both. The paid tier gives access to Pro/2 Pro models (higher quality, higher resolution), but the watermark is still there.

Banana Clean handles all versions with the same approach: reverse alpha blending with the appropriate mask selected by image dimensions. Processing under 100ms regardless of model version.

If Google changes the watermark in future models, updated masks would be shipped through the Chrome Web Store. The approach (reverse alpha blending of a known static watermark) will work as long as Google uses alpha compositing.
